description:
1186 Route 130 is a premier ±3.23-acre redevelopment opportunity positioned along one of Robbinsville’s most trafficked commercial corridors. The property offers outstanding visibility, direct Route 130 frontage, and immediate connectivity to the New Jersey Turnpike, making it highly attractive for logistics, retail, service, and flex users.

The site benefits from an adopted Redevelopment Plan that streamlines approvals and allows for a versatile commercial program. The plan supports the construction of a proposed ±32,056-square-foot warehouse space with ±400 square feet of office, plus an additional ±6,596 square feet of retail or office space.

The Sellers have secured the Redevelopment Plan, which provides the zoning necessary for the proposed concept. With zoning already in place, the Buyer must apply to the Township to be designated as the Redeveloper and can then submit an as-of-right plan for approval.
The Redevelopment Plan also permits a wide range of revenue-diversified uses, including retail, office, indoor recreation, flex/wholesale, and showroom-type businesses—providing exceptional flexibility for developers and end users.

Robbinsville is one of Mercer County’s most dynamic logistics submarkets, driven by strong demand from 3PLs, e-commerce operators, and proximity to major infrastructure. Since Amazon’s fulfillment center opened in 2014, the area has rapidly evolved into a high-performing logistics and commercial hub, attracting new businesses and supporting consistent retail activity.

1186 Route 130 offers a rare chance to develop within a business-friendly community experiencing sustained growth, rising property values, and long-term demand for both industrial and consumer-facing uses.

Jacklene serves as Vice Chair and provides Investment Sales & Advisory expertise to  institutional and private investors. She focuses on all major product types including Office, Industrial, Retail, Residential/Mixed Use and Land. She has vast experience in working with Special Servicers (Loan and REO teams), Asset Mangers and other Fidicuciary Agents on CMBS and balance sheet properties, and other type of distressed assets. She is also very active with marketing and selling properties via online auctions, most notable Tenx.com. 

Jacklene joined  the firm in 2013. Prior to Colliers, she was a Director for the NKF Capital Markets Tri-State Team from 2008 to 2013.  She has been a top 10% producer for Colliers from 2014 to current year and recognized for this achievement by being part of the prestigious Everest Club.

description:
The South Campus comprises 120, 140, and 160 High Street, as well as 360 Union Street, in Springfield, Massachusetts. 
 
The South Campus property was originally built in 1906 as the Wesson Memorial Hospital, with several additions added and approximately 185,000 square feet on eight floors. The property has approximately 675 parking spaces in the eight parking lots surrounding the campus. The property is currently 32.48% leased to various uses, with the majority being medical. It is also home to government and social service agencies vital to the community, including Baystate Health, one of Western Massachusetts's most vital economic engines, delivering over $2.6 billion in yearly economic impact.
 
The property is located in Springfield, Massachusetts, the state's third-largest city, often described as the Crossroads of New England for its unique role as the primary shipping nexus connecting New York City, Boston, Montreal, and the Great Lakes region through Albany, New York. As a strategic geographical trade center, Springfield offers investors more than just proximity to major metropolitan areas; it benefits from its position alongside the Connecticut River, which has historically facilitated commerce and transportation. The city's robust infrastructure includes extensive rail lines and several interstate highways, notably Interstate 90 (Mass Pike) and Interstate 91, which link Springfield directly to key cities such as New Haven, Hartford, Holyoke, Northampton, and Vermont. In addition, one of the few spurs of Interstate 91 in Massachusetts, Interstate 291, runs through Springfield, providing a valuable secondary connection between Interstate 90 and Interstate 91. This exceptional connectivity not only enhances access for tenants and visitors but also underpins the city's enduring appeal for commercial investment, making the property an attractive option for investors seeking strong logistical advantages and growth potential in a vibrant, economically significant region.
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

brokerDetails.name: Michael Guidicelli, CCIM, SIOR
brokerDetails.tagLine: Your Trusted Commercial Real Estate Advisor
brokerDetails.title: Broker & Owner
brokerDetails.bio:
Michael stands at the forefront of the investment real estate market as the esteemed Owner and Power Broker of Regions Commercial, LLC. His firm is renowned for its specialization in the sale of intricate investment properties and providing unparalleled tenant representation services. Michael's expertise is backed by his prestigious designation as a CCIM (Certified Commercial Investment Member), a testament to his deep-rooted knowledge and practical experience in commercial and investment real estate.

Throughout his illustrious career, Michael has played a pivotal role in shaping the landscape of the real estate industry. His dedication and leadership have been instrumental in his decade-long service on the Board of Directors for the Connecticut Chapter of CCIM, including two remarkable years as President (2016 & 2017). Under his stewardship, the chapter witnessed exponential growth, nearly doubling its membership and tripling its net operating income. His exceptional leadership skills were further recognized through his role as Regional Vice President of Region 11, overseeing pivotal chapters across the Northeastern United States.

Michael's commitment to excellence is also evident in his attainment of the SIOR designation from The SOCIETY OF INDUSTRIAL AND OFFICE REALTORS®, marking him as a leading authority in commercial real estate brokerage. His tenure as President of the CT/W. MA SIOR Chapter and his continued role as Immediate Past President highlight his significant contributions and steadfast dedication to the industry.

His outstanding achievements have not gone unnoticed, earning him a spot in the top 40 Under 40 by the Hartford Business Journal, and highlighting his remarkable impact in the field. Furthermore, Michael's academic credentials are equally impressive, with a graduate degree from Harvard Law School's Program on Negotiation, equipping him with unrivaled negotiation skills that benefit his clients immensely.

As a staunch advocate for his clients, Michael offers expert guidance through every step of the real estate transaction process. His commitment to his clients is unparalleled, offering both commission-based and consulting services tailored to meet their unique needs. The opportunity to begin with an initial consultation without obligation is a testament to his client-centered approach, ensuring a comprehensive understanding of their requirements from the outset.

Michael's distinguished career, marked by his leadership roles, prestigious designations, and unwavering dedication to his clients, sets him apart as a leading figure in the investment real estate industry.

description:
Set in beautiful Wailuku, Hawaii, 109 Waiko Road stands out as a prime commercial development opportunity. Spanning 8.55 acres of fully graded land, the property offers a rare blend of flexible zoning and established infrastructure, making it exceptionally well-suited for a broad array of commercial ventures. The site is subdivided into seven graded lots, each benefiting from paved roadways and utility connections for electric, water, and telephone services. This thoughtful preparation, paired with completed Archaeological Preservation Plans, streamlines the development process and reduces lead time for construction.
 
The property’s zoning creates an array of possibilities. Approximately 2.8 acres along Waiko Road are eligible for business district projects, supporting diverse uses such as retail, restaurants, educational facilities, and office spaces. The remaining acreage is zoned for light industrial applications, including warehousing, light manufacturing, automotive operations, and distribution. This versatility offers developers the freedom to conceptualize projects that align with market needs and maximize the site’s potential.
 
Strategically positioned just 10 minutes from Downtown Kahului and centrally located between Honoapiilani Highway and Kuihelani Highway, 109 Waiko Road provides easy regional connectivity. The property enjoys visibility to more than 7,300 vehicles each day, a significant advantage for operations that benefit from high traffic exposure, such as retail and dining establishments. Proximity to local attractions like the Maui Tropical Plantation and the King Kamehameha Golf Club further expands the prospective customer base, supporting sustained business growth.
 
Wailuku’s commercial environment amplifies the property’s potential. Within a 5-mile radius, the population surpasses 54,000, with an average household income exceeding $107,000. Annual consumer spending in the area is more than $589 million, underscoring the region’s economic vitality. A workforce of over 36,800 supports more than 3,800 businesses locally, providing a strong labor pool for new tenants. This combination of favorable demographics and business activity makes the site a compelling option for developers seeking to tap into the region’s strong demand and spending power.
 
A strategic location, established infrastructure, and dynamic market conditions position 109 Waiko Road as an outstanding foundation for developers seeking to deliver high-quality commercial or industrial projects in central Maui.
 
Property tours are by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
Claim a historic piece of Downtown Marshalltown, Iowa, with this incredible opportunity to acquire 11 E Church Street. Offering a landmark office property that is currently approximately 59% occupied with quality tenants, this valuable opportunity presents in-place rental revenue alongside substantial value-add growth potential for investors. 
 
The property includes the historic Ford building, once a dealership, assembly, and distribution center that has been preserved as a multi-tenant office building spanning 38,450 square feet. Careful preservation and recent upgrades, including a roof replacement in 2019, updated plumbing, electrical, HVAC, a full sprinkler fire suppression system, and a 200-kW standby generator, ensure a clean, quiet, and efficient work environment suitable for a diverse range of professional uses. The opportunity also includes an adjacent 0.36-acre parking lot, providing ample on-site parking and potential for future expansion. 
 
Situated within downtown’s designated Opportunity Zone, 11 E Church Street delivers lucrative possibilities for long-term redevelopment or adaptive reuse. Qualifying projects will be able to leverage substantial capital gains tax incentives, significantly offsetting development costs. Positioned at a high-traffic signalized intersection, the location delivers premier downtown visibility to heavy vehicle and foot traffic. Additionally, the site is easily reachable for commuters across the greater region, placed just off Highway 14 and less than 10 minutes from Highway 30. The surrounding area presents a walkable hub of activity, delivering quick access to quality retail, restaurants, and recreation amenities. 
 
Marshalltown’s strong demographics and solid leasing fundamentals underscore the property’s investment appeal. Within 5 miles, the area boasts over 29,000 residents and a workforce exceeding 20,800, generating more than $317 million in annual consumer spending. This robust business climate fuels high demand for quality office space, with vacancies at only 3.1% and no new office developments in the past decade. The resulting shortage of functional workspace has driven consistent rent growth, averaging 2.6% annually over the last five years. These factors combine to create a unique opportunity for investors or businesses to acquire a historic asset with proven income and significant future upside.
 
Property tours are by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

description:
12611 Smoketown Road is an ideal parcel for investors and developers to bring their vision to life in Woodbridge, Virginia. 
 
This property presents a prime opportunity for the development of up to 15,000 square feet of retail or medical office space. The land has excellent visibility and frontage on Smoketown Road, which bodes well for developers. Formerly site plan-approved as a retail pad, the lot is construction-ready with already in-place utilities and stormwater infrastructure, minimizing development lead time. With ample parking and excellent road visibility, 12611 Smoketown Road is well-positioned to support a range of commercial or healthcare-oriented uses. Proffers govern certain use restrictions, including prohibitions on alcohol sales, churches, and select retail categories (such as greeting cards, beauty supply, and prepackaged foods) to avoid competition with the adjacent Walgreens. Prospective buyers should review the proffers to confirm compatibility with their intended use. 12611 Smoketown Road is an ideal location for medical practices, clinics, or service-based retail in a high-traffic, growing corridor.
 
Strategically located along Smoketown Road, this property benefits from its proximity to major highways, including Interstate 95, ensuring seamless connectivity to Northern Virginia and the Greater Washington, DC, area. The site is surrounded by a dense and growing residential community with over 220,000 residents within 5 miles and an average household income exceeding $150,000, as well as popular commercial attractions such as Potomac Mills Mall and Stonebridge at Potomac Town Center. With a strong mix of national retailers, dining options, and entertainment venues nearby, the area enjoys significant foot traffic and a robust customer base. 12611 Smoketown Road's premier location positions businesses at the center of a thriving retail hub, ready to capitalize on the area's dynamic growth.
 
This property enjoys advantages from being situated within the greater Washington, DC area. The region consistently draws skilled professionals from around the nation thanks to its diverse economy, excellent quality of life, and lively cultural atmosphere.

description:
The subject property is the remaining portion of the Harbour Isle Community, a waterfront development project at the northern end of the peninsula. The project is a world class community with in-place amenities including a community beach club recreation center containing a fitness center, meeting space, private dining/bar, a kayak launch, lap pool, manicured landscaping and a vast nature trail surrounding the tranquil community lake. Additionally, there are a Compass Hotel, Floridays Restaurant, and Safe Harbor wet/dry slip marina located just outside the gates of the development. Located along Manatee Ave. W. and just minutes from the public beach, the remaining parcel is a fully waterfront pad with expansive views reaching out to the Skyway Bridge. The remaining phase consists of 3 parcels totaling 18.90 buildable acres. The road to the site has been graded but not yet paved. The existing project already consists of 411 low rise townhomes along the spine with short term rentals beside the marina and is ready for someone to finish building this third phase. The Seller is looking for the right Buyer/Developer to finish the project with a mixture of high-end condos, townhomes or single family residences.
  
Please note the following buying terms to be included in an LOI:
(i) Buyer’s intended use for the property
(ii)  assumption/payment of contingent fee obligations owed to Hayden Lane
(iii) assumption of declarant rights under Master Declaration and control over Beach Club and other common areas and amenities (as is, where-is, without recourse back to Minto)
(iv) construction of Martinique Drive extension; and 
(v) property will be added to Master HOA.

description:
Positioned at one of South Los Angeles' most active commercial intersections, 7210 S. Western Avenue presents a compelling development opportunity — immediate commercial construction with RTI permits, and a clear path to 67 multifamily units on a 13,200 SF parcel.

The site comes with ready-to-issue permits for a 3,750 SF single-story commercial build, enabling swift delivery and early cash flow. C2-2D-CPIO zoning supports the full spectrum of high-demand uses — retail, restaurant, medical office, urgent care, cannabis dispensary, banking, and mixed-use — offering the optionality to attract a wide tenant base from day one.

For developers pursuing multifamily, the site presents a clear path to up to 67 units — including approximately 57 market rate — via California's State Density Bonus Law (AB 1287 double-stack), or 54 units through the City's Transit Oriented Communities (TOC) program. Both programs unlock increased FAR, with reduced parking requirements and streamlined approval pathways — creating exceptional density. The property sits within the South LA CPIO's Western Avenue TOD subarea, one of the city's designated transit corridors for accelerated multifamily development.

Positioned at a signalized intersection with 120 feet of Western Avenue frontage, the site commands strong visibility and consistent foot and vehicle traffic. USC, SoFi Stadium, and the Kia Forum are minutes away, anchoring a trade area with deep consumer demand and rising residential density.

Los Angeles is the nation's second-largest metropolitan economy and a global hub for entertainment, technology, international trade, and healthcare — consistently ranking among the top U.S. markets for real estate investment and capital deployment. South Los Angeles in particular is undergoing meaningful revitalization, with infrastructure investment and transit expansion driving renewed developer interest along the Western Avenue corridor.

description:
1141 E Kemper Road welcomes investors and developers to develop a 7.639-acre commercial parcel in a northern Cincinnati commercial thoroughfare. 
    
The subject land site comes with access off East Kemper Road and an access easement to Century Boulevard, with all utilities to the site. 1141 E Kemper Road is zoned Office Business, which allows a range of commercial uses, including office, school, church, government, medical-ambulatory care, and veterinary care. Commercial developments surround the land parcel, including Tri-County Mall, Springdale Plaza, Towne Center, and Kemper Square. As the city's primary shopping, dining, and entertainment corridor, Kemper Road sees daily traffic of more than 23,800 vehicles. 
    
Residents of the Springdale suburb often visit the Hilda-Ross Memorial Park (2 miles) for outdoor recreation, enjoy a round of golf at the Glenview Golf Course (3 miles), and are about 20 miles from both Great American Ball Park and Paycor Stadium. Home of the Cincinnati Reds, Great American Ball Park offers over 40,000 seats. In contrast, Paycor Stadium — home of the Cincinnati Bengals — features more than 60,000 fans cheering on their favorite players in the stands. The Springdale Community Center features several amenities, including an Olympic-sized pool and splash pad, and hosts local events throughout the year.
    
The 5-mile demographics confirm a growing population of 141,269 residents, with average household income exceeding $98,500 and robust spending power of more than $1.8 billion annually. Entertainment, food and beverages, household goods, and transportation represent the realm’s largest spending categories, aligning with Kemper Road’s development and consumer demand.

description:
Prime Developer exclusively presents the commercial lots of the Rio Ranch neighborhood development. The tracts are located in Williamson County and centered around the intersection of Ronald Reagan Boulevard and CR 258, boasting ideal elevations to take in scenic views. Rio Ranch’s commercial lots are zoned for business, Liberty Hill ISD, and are ideal for a broad range of uses to serve the new single-family homes being built around them. 
  
As more established communities nearby, like Georgetown, Leander, and Cedar Park, continue outward growth, Liberty Hill is on the cutting edge of the Austin metro’s expansion. Reaching approximately 15,000 residents in 2025, Liberty Hill’s population nearly tripled since 2020. Rio Ranch’s strategic location along the area’s signature corridor, Ronald Reagan Boulevard, is poised to elevate the success of any establishment. There are no commercial destinations along the thoroughfare for 3.5 miles when it intersects with Highway 29, making this a valuable opportunity to exclusively serve the hundreds of new households coming to the area. Additionally, this locale’s proximity to major employers has elevated personal incomes well above the national average, contributing to over $753,500,000 in annual consumer spending within a 5-mile radius in 2024. 
 
Build a fixture of this flourishing area and take advantage of its superior demographics with a Rio Ranch commercial lot today.

executiveSummary:
Prime Developer exclusively presents the commercial lots of the Rio Ranch neighborhood development. The tracts are located in Williamson County and centered around the intersection of Ronald Reagan Boulevard and CR 258, boasting ideal elevations to take in scenic views. Rio Ranch’s commercial lots are zoned for business and Liberty Hill Independent School District (ISD) and are ideal for a broad range of uses to serve the new single-family homes being built around them. 
     
With more established nearby communities like Georgetown, Leander, and Cedar Park continuing outward growth, Liberty Hill is on the cutting edge of the Austin metro’s expansion. Reaching approximately 15,000 residents in 2025, Liberty Hill’s population nearly tripled since 2020. Rio Ranch’s strategic location along the area’s signature corridor, Ronald Reagan Boulevard, is poised to elevate the success of any establishment. There are no commercial destinations along the thoroughfare for 3.5 miles when it intersects with Highway 29, making this a valuable opportunity to exclusively serve the hundreds of new households coming to the area. Additionally, this locale’s proximity to major employers has elevated personal incomes well above the national average, contributing to over $753,500,000 in annual consumer spending within a 5-mile radius in 2024. 
     
Build a fixture of this growing area and take advantage of its superior demographics with a Rio Ranch commercial lot today.

description:
This exceptional industrial land for sale in Perris presents a rare owner/user opportunity and shovel-ready development site. Located at Tobacco Road, the subject property consists of approximately 4.54 acres (±197,762 SF) of fully paved and fully fenced I-P zoned land, with CZ 7830 pre-application review completed, within unincorporated Riverside County (APN 317-270-002). The site features a secured perimeter, a high-quality asphalt-paved surface, and on-site utilities, making it pad-ready and ready for immediate occupancy. Ideally positioned as a freeway-adjacent location with a pre-approved PAR230005, the property offers significant build-to-suit potential. With substantial frontage of approximately 297 feet on Tobacco Road and 654 feet on Water Avenue, this turnkey industrial yard supports efficient truck parking, vehicle storage, and outdoor storage operations alongside traditional logistics needs.
      
Zoned I-P Industrial Park zoning (Zoned I-P under CZ 7830). With a zoning change completed, A-1-1 to I-P, this site offers by-right development potential and a streamlined approval process. The site is intended for warehouse development and allows a broad range of logistics uses, including distribution center operations, outdoor storage facilities, yard storage, cold storage, mini-warehouses, self-storage, and light manufacturing. This zoning is subject to Industrial Park Plot Plan approval, and certain specialty uses may require a CUP (Conditional Use Permit).
     
Infrastructure is positioned to support near-term development with all utilities at the site, as water mains are available at the property boundary along Tobacco Road and Water Avenue. A sewer main runs adjacent to Water Avenue, and power and telecom are located at or near the site.
     
Development groundwork is favorable. The site features a fully paved asphalt surface and is completely fenced and secured with gated access, presenting a shovel-ready, turnkey industrial yard. A Riverside County PAR230005 preapplication review completed evaluated future industrial use, including a 4,800-square-foot warehouse and truck parking with full improvements. The property previously completed a zoning change from A-1-1 to I-P under CZ 7830. These existing improvements make the site ready for immediate truck parking operations within a secured fenced perimeter, reinforcing suitability for fleet parking, trailer storage, RV storage, and Inland Empire industrial real estate development while streamlining a buyer’s entitlement pathway as a value-add investment offering lot assemblage opportunities with adjacent parcels. 
      
This institutional-grade location offers immediate access to the I-215 logistics corridor, connecting directly to State Route 60 and to Interstates 10 and 15. Notable regional destinations include Ontario International Airport, 30 miles from the site; the Ports of LA/Long Beach, 68 miles away; and Downtown Riverside, approximately 18 miles away. Surrounded by established logistics parks, the location is ideal for last-mile delivery operators, vehicle storage, equipment yard users, regional distributors, and light manufacturing users requiring direct freeway access.

description:
RCR Taylor Rail Logistics Park presents the opportunity to acquire industrial development sites with sweeping transport capabilities in highly sought-after Central Texas. 
  
This nearly fully claimed 750-acre park features rail-served lots capable of receiving buildings from 50,000 to 1,000,000 square feet and is poised to become a significant factor in the Austin MSA. The rail park also features Class I Dual Rail Service by Union Pacific and BNSF. Park services include Manifest and Unit Train Rail service, rail car storage, transloading, commodity storage, rail car switching, auto ramps, controlled access, and security. Oncor provides electrical service of 20 megawatts, with 300 megawatts of excess capacity from a 138 kVA transmission line within a quarter-mile of the site. Additionally, there are three robust fiber optic providers. 
  
The Austin Market is a preferred region for distributors and manufacturers due to its proximity to all major metros throughout the state. The cities that define the Texas Triangle, San Antonio, Dallas/Fort Worth, and Houston, are within a 2.5-hour drive. Transport destinations like College Station, Corpus Christi, Killeen, or Waco are easily reached from the park. 
 
RCR Taylor Rail Logistics Park sits along Highway 79, offering streamlined access to Texas 130 and Interstate 35. Transporters can reach Mexico in about 4.5 hours. The area also provides a vast workforce, with the trade, transportation, and utilities sector being the second-largest industry by employment within a 10-mile radius. Major organizations have set up industrial facilities here, like Samsung’s semiconductor plant that borders the park, due to the area's premier connectivity and accessible labor.

description:
1701 W Bonds Ranch Road in Fort Worth, Texas, is a zoning-free 20-acre tract in one of the fastest-growing sections of Tarrant County. The property sits directly across from Wellington, a well-established residential community, and just east of major planned new developments at Bonds Ranch and Boat Club Roads. Adding even more momentum to the area's growth, the brand-new Eagle Mountain-Saginaw Independent School District high school is only minutes to the west.
 
This property offers 987 feet of W Bonds Ranch Road frontage and expansion potential through additional off-market land (contact for details), allowing buyers to pursue larger development plans. 1701 W Bonds Ranch Road's placement outside the Fort Worth city limits and outside extraterritorial jurisdiction (ETJ) makes the parcel especially unique, free of zoning restrictions, city oversight, and deed restrictions. A rare opportunity to begin with a blank canvas for uses like a sporting complex, flex space, office, retail, warehousing, distribution, medical, mixed-use, and more.
 
The land is relatively flat and near city services, checking off the boxes for development. 1701 W Bonds Ranch Road can also be divided into two approximately 10-acre parcels. Accessibility will only improve with W Bonds Ranch Road set to expand from two lanes to six lanes, plus the planned Willow Springs Road extension along the east boundary, running north and south, giving the property dual road frontage.
 
1701 W Bonds Ranch Road presents wide-open potential for an owner/user, investor, or developer in a community that's hungry for commercial space. Approximately 135,172 residents live within 5 miles and represent an average household income of nearly $133,000. Restaurants, retail, and entertainment are in high demand here to serve the area's rapidly growing residential base that is expanding by 4.7% per year. 
 
From flex-space warehouses to outdoor storage sites, Avondale Development Group is an experienced developer in Fort Worth, Texas. The firm is selling the raw land, but can offer general contracting services for vertical and horizontal improvements, engineering, architecture, and permitting. Please contact a listing agent for more details, or to see additional on and off-market properties within proximity of this location.

description:
Discover the possibilities of this valuable high-density tract as new developments and swelling demographics elevate demand in the Front Range. 
  
6615 29th Street is a 2.51-acre site with an optimal elevation to maximize sprawling views of the city and mountains to watch as this locale continues its rapid growth. Developers can get to work right away with utilities stubbed to the site, no detention pond required, and a city motivated to encourage new builds. The City of Greeley’s relaxed zoning ordinance affords immense flexibility in development, allowing for ideal uses like hotels, retail centers, restaurants, offices, medical centers, and more. 
  
The prior user proposed a four-story hotel that was approved by the city. Construction documents of the proposed project are available to view in the Data Room. Since the plan was created, zoning codes were changed in favor of this site, and developers can now build up to 60 feet, allowing for up to five stories and about 120 rooms or a podium hotel with a ground-floor restaurant. Environmental reports and surveys have also been conducted and are available in the Data Room. 
  
Situated at a roundabout that centers UCHealth Greeley Hospital, several retail shops, an apartment complex, and a single-family subdivision, 6615 29th Street is poised to anchor this buzzing node. A new 270-unit apartment community and neighborhood are underway in the vicinity, underpinning the rising real estate investment here. The Colorado Eagles, a farm team for the Avalanche NHL team, is finalizing plans to move its arena to a proposed $1.1 billion sports and entertainment complex in west Greeley. The project would be a few minutes down the highway and would also include a hotel and water park in addition to the new arena and publicly accessible hockey facilities, elevating the success of surrounding businesses. 
  
Development interest in the area has been driven by significant migration to West Greeley as the population and personal incomes rise. executiveSummary:
Discover the possibilities of this valuable high-density tract as new developments and swelling demographics elevate demand in the Front Range. 
  
6615 29th Street is a 2.51-acre site with an optimal elevation to maximize sprawling views of the city and mountains to watch as this locale continues its rapid growth. Developers can get to work right away with utilities stubbed to the site, no detention pond required, and a city motivated to encourage new builds. The City of Greeley’s relaxed zoning ordinance affords immense flexibility in development, allowing for ideal uses like hotels, retail centers, restaurants, offices, medical centers, and more. 
  
The prior user proposed a four-story hotel that was approved by the city. Construction documents of the proposed project are available to view in the Data Room. Since the plan was created, zoning codes were changed in favor of this site, and developers can now build up to 60 feet, allowing for up to five stories and about 120 rooms or a podium hotel with a ground-floor restaurant. Environmental reports and surveys have also been conducted and are available in the Data Room. 
  
Situated at a roundabout that centers UCHealth Greeley Hospital, several retail shops, an apartment complex, and a single-family subdivision, 6615 29th Street is poised to anchor this buzzing node. A new 270-unit apartment community and neighborhood are underway in the vicinity, underpinning the rising real estate investment here. address: Blue Mound Rd & John Day Rd
description:
Build upon the flourishing Alliance corridor with these valuable land sites in Haslet, Texas, presented by Westwood Real Estate Development. 
  
This 97-acre tract sits at the hard corner of Blue Mound and John Day Road, featuring divisible lots from 1 to 20 acres, with pricing scaled by acreage purchased. The shovel-ready land has easy access to utilities, no zoning or ETJ restrictions. This opportunity is also not subject to city taxes. Three schools and thousands of residences exist or are underway in the vicinity of the property, and the flexibility in potential projects grants developers unlimited creativity in weaving a unique development into this abounding area. Ideal for community driven businesses that would provide convenience and support to the thousands of residents and schools surrounding this location. Light industrial, small bay flex, medical, corner store, restaurant, dry cleaner, etc.  Grow with the community! Also, suited for larger group with warehouse/industrial use. Being on the rail and less than 10 minutes from the Intermodal, this property is ideal for those transporting from across the country.
  
Located 3.5 miles from Interstate 35W, the tract is in close proximity to the Alliance corridor’s primary artery, offering easy access to the area's economic drivers. Development is roaring, as over 12 million square feet of commercial space has been delivered within a 5-mile radius since 2020. These come amid a growing backdrop of affluent communities with an average household income of $129,176 annually in that same radius. 
  
AllianceTexas is a growing, master-planned development spanning over 27,000 acres with 58 million square feet of mixed-use development. It has generated an estimated $120 billion in economic impact for the North Texas region. This monumental project has fostered more nearby developments, forming the Alliance area or corridor stretching from just north of Loop 820 to Denton County. Some of the area's anchors include Alliance Airport, a data center hub, Circle T Ranch, Alliance Town Center, and the Heritage neighborhood. The 97-acre tract is situated in market-shaping developments, positioning it well for any future project to become a go-to destination for the region.

description:
As of March 25th, 2026, our new main entrance and roundabouts are 70% completed! The roundabouts and new entrance are expected to be complete by the end of May. With this milestone, pad sites are now officially for sale. 
  
Currently, the Ambassador Development at KCI includes a 300,000-square-foot high-end office building home to industry leaders such as Purina, National Beef Packing Co., Boilermakers International, Western Governors University, and TSA. Lots from 1 acre and up are ready for buyers looking to capitalize on sites well-suited for hotels, medical facilities, auto dealerships, dine-in restaurants, convenience stores, offices, and quick-service restaurants.
  
If customization is necessary, the developer will consider build-to-suit sites to meet specific needs, creating strong synergy in an area that already mixes hospitality, office, and industrial uses. The property offers more than 2,500 linear feet of frontage along Interstate 29 and 2,100 linear feet along Highway 435, in a corridor that includes hotels such as Hilton and Holiday Inn, as well as healthcare services from TriWest Healthcare Alliance. The Ambassador Development at KCI is located a few miles west of the newly announced 5.5 million-square-foot data center and directly south of Hunt Midwest’s planned 3,300-acre logistics park. Platte County is an ideal location for business, recognized as the healthiest, wealthiest, and fastest-growing county in Missouri. Businesses are encouraged to become part of this exciting development.
  
Buyers also benefit from proximity to the new CVS distribution center, the country's largest Westlake Hardware distribution center, and Zona Rosa, an open-air mixed-use center with upscale retail, restaurants, entertainment, office space, and luxury residential. Just a 20-minute drive to Downtown Kansas City and adjacent to the new Kansas City International Airport, this site is positioned to become the next major master-planned destination at KCI.

brokerDetails.name: Michael Meier
brokerDetails.title: Operating Partner
brokerDetails.bio:
Michael Meier is the Principal and Co-Founder of Aristocrat Realty & Development. After two decades as an entrepreneur, he moved from running a local retail business into real estate with a simple goal: deliver better service. Under his leadership, Aristocrat Realty has grown to a 34-agent team. The Kansas City Business Journal recognized Michael as the metro’s #1 retail broker for 2022-2024, and over the last three years, he has facilitated more than $90 million in transactions.

He specializes in off-market opportunities and commercial development across medical, office, industrial, and mixed-use projects. Michael also serves the community as a KCMO Parks Commissioner, a member of the KCMO Board of Zoning Adjustment, and Chair of the Northland Chamber of Commerce Planning & Development Committee. He serves on the boards of the Platte County Economic Development Council, Platte County Senior Services, and the Northland Workforce Development Center.

Away from work, Michael enjoys tailgating and cheering on the Chiefs and Royals, as well as spending time with his family—his daughter Julia, baby Wrenley, and his wife, Kailey. He supports organizations such as Children’s Mercy Hospital, Ronald McDonald House, and the Veterans Community Project.

description:
This 17-property portfolio offers investors the opportunity to expand their holdings by purchasing individual locations, select groupings of similar properties, or the entire portfolio. This nationwide property offering is facilitated by NEWAMCO.ORG. The seller group aims to divest certain assets to realign its portfolio towards targeted sectors.
  
The properties in this portfolio range from a 2,575-square-foot former Taco Bell to a former 55,000-square-foot big-box Academy Sports and development land, showcasing a diverse array of assets. The built property types include former CVS Pharmacy and Pep Boys automotive repair buildings, complemented by two big-box stores and two restaurants. Each property is freestanding, standalone, and vacant and benefits from flexible zoning and not being part of other shopping centers, thus offering maximum versatility. Certain properties feature unique attributes such as drive-thrus, restaurant equipment, and drive-in doors, which enhance their adaptability for various future uses. In addition, the micro-locational benefits make them attractive to a wide range of buyers, with the corresponding cities and markets performing exceptionally well. The larger acreage commercial tracts boast excellent frontage along strong retail corridors with new developments coming around them. Two strongly positioned land parcels present dynamic development opportunities.

The wide blend of property offerings presents ample 1031 READY exchange options in price, geography and industry sector opportunities.
  
The markets surrounding each property show solid signs for successful investments, supported by multifaceted economies that promote diverse uses. More than half of the properties are located on hard corners, and some are in designated opportunity zones. 
  
For more information, please refer to the attached documents and contact Randall Perry at 727-755-3249.

description:
Prime development lots are now available at The Brands, Northern Colorado’s premier mixed-use destination, with sites ranging from 1.62 to 23.45 acres. Strategically located along Interstate 25 at Crossroads Boulevard, The Brands spans over 200 acres, supporting various uses, including retail, office, medical, hospitality, and light industrial. This high-visibility corridor is experiencing rapid growth, with exceptional regional access and infrastructure.
  
The development includes The Brands at the Ranch and Brands West, located on 60 and 140 acres, respectively, on either side of Interstate 25. Together, they will bring over 2.6 million square feet of commercial space to the region, anchored by attractions such as the Galaxy IMAX Theatre, Olympic-sized pool complex, twin ice rinks, luxury multifamily, and walkable retail. Across from The Ranch Events Complex, which hosts over 2,000 events annually, the location benefits from year-round traffic and energy.
  
Brands East, located just off Crossroads Boulevard in Windsor, offers additional development potential near the future Bass Pro Shops, scheduled to open in late 2025. With shovel-ready sites, regional draw, and flexible parcel sizes, The Brands offers a rare opportunity to grow within one of Northern Colorado’s most dynamic corridors.

description:
Join the City of Westminster as the master developer in its ambitious initiative to transform its downtown into a preeminent destination for the town’s residents and the Denver metro. 
  
The Downtown Westminster project is in full swing, with several new developments, including Westminster Row, Townhomes on Harlan, Origin Hotel, Alamo Drafthouse, and others, already thriving here. This brought the district over 1,000 apartment units, 34 townhomes, 125 hotel rooms, and more than 120,000 square feet of fresh retail space. Another set of townhomes is also underway, and a 220-unit active adult community is scheduled to open in 2025.
  
This project was designed with a bustling pedestrian atmosphere in mind. Manicured green space will envelop most sides of the district, with bike lanes and paths sprawled throughout. The development is planned around Center Park, which the city will start in October 2023. Access to Downtown Westminster is a breeze via an underpass connecting to the US 36/Sheridan bus station, a newly built 885-car public parking garage, and an incoming FasTracks commuter rail station, inviting visitors to shop, play, and stay awhile. 
  
Given the planned and existing developments, the city aims to sell these tracts for office, condo, and active senior living developments, with some consideration for multifamily communities. The buildings must be at least eight stories tall to achieve Downtown Westminster's desired density and actively support the other developments. 
  
Situated directly off Highway 36, builders would be hard-pressed to find a better-connected location with supporting infrastructure in the Denver region. Boulder and Downtown Denver are virtually the same distance away in terms of driving time, and this opportunity fills a long-overdue development that will cater to the modern consumer between the two hubs. Throughout the Denver metro area and the broader United States, demand for trendy, mixed-use districts that enable residents and professionals to establish quick connections with one another, both where they live and work, has rapidly increased. There are numerous examples where this model has proven successful, with vacancy rates lower than those of most market peers, thanks to abundant amenities and streamlined transportation conduits that support offices and living communities. Downtown Westminster has an advantage as it is closer to significant hubs, and demographic trends show a younger and faster-growing pool of residents in the area. The number of households within a 1-mile radius grew by 44.2% from 2010 to 2023, and the median age of that cohort is 37.4. Building upon the success of its predecessors, Downtown Westminster intends to do it again - and do it better.
  
Westminster has three opportunity zones: Downtown Westminster, Historic Westminster, and Westminster Station TOD. Enacted as part of the 2017 tax reform package (Tax Cuts and Jobs Act), Opportunity Zones address uneven economic recovery and persistent lack of growth that have left many communities behind. The purpose of the Opportunity Zone program is to encourage private capital investment in low-income communities.

Bill, a Managing Principal at The Laramie Company, has worn many hats in the commercial real estate industry since 1982. Heading up retail/office transactions, tenant/buyer representation, land sales, property and asset management, Bill’s expertise in managing organizational dynamics combined with deal making has achieved tangible and profitable results for his clients.

Currently leasing all of the retail space in Downtown Westminster for four separate developers in mixed-use projects, Bill and his team have achieved over 50% pre-leasing within 75,000 square feet of first generation retail space. Also leasing the retail portions of McGregor Square, a project under development by The Colorado Rockies, Bill and his team recently signed The Tattered Cover Book Store as an anchor tenant. Bill is well versed in office leasing and asset management, transacting space within the historic Equitable Building and other select office properties.

description:
The parcel of land on Feather River Boulevard is an ideal place for investors and developers to make their mark in Oroville, California. 
 
This expansive 5.12-acre parcel, now fully cleared and featuring an existing parking lot, offers a blank canvas for visionary investors and developers. The previous structure has been entirely removed, streamlining the path to new construction. Zoned C-2 General Commercial, this property in Oroville, California, is ideally suited for a diverse array of uses, including retail, service-oriented businesses, offices, and light industrial operations that cater to the immediate area and the broader community. The C-2 zoning is specifically intended to create accessible, highly functional spaces for thriving commercial activity. With its generous size, strategic location, and flexible zoning, this site provides investors with a unique opportunity to shape and energize Oroville’s commercial landscape while making a lasting impact on the local economy.
 
This site in Oroville is an excellent opportunity for developers seeking strong visibility and accessibility. The property features dual frontage on both Feather River Boulevard and Route 70, a vital connector with significant daily traffic that links different parts of town. This high-traffic location enhances the potential for commercial development. Oroville offers residents a relaxed, nature-oriented lifestyle, attracting both locals and visitors. Nearby attractions such as Lake Oroville and the Feather River provide abundant outdoor recreation, adding to the area’s appeal. The property is also conveniently close to the Oroville Municipal Airport and Northwest Lineman College, making it ideal for service-oriented businesses and facilities catering to students, travelers, and professionals. Various national retailers and local businesses surround the parcel, including Starbucks, CVS, KFC, Grocery Outlet, Fosters Freeze, McDonald’s, and several hotels. This diverse mix creates a dynamic environment and ensures steady customer traffic. With national brands and local favorites nearby, the site offers a versatile foundation for new development and is well-suited for retail, office, or service-based projects in this growing community.
 
Property tours are available by appointment only. Please contact the listing broker to schedule.

Ken Noack, Jr. is a land and retail specialist with Newmark in Sacramento, California and serves as a Senior Managing Director. Noack has a diversified background in the real estate industry. description:
Unlock the potential of this exceptional 10.86-acre property in Troy, Illinois, an outstanding investment and development property that combines location, scale, and flexibility for maximum impact. 
 
Boasting prime frontage on Edwardsville Road, one of the region’s most trafficked retail corridors, businesses will benefit from remarkable daily traffic counts. Fully serviced with gas, water, and sewer, this land is development-ready, saving time and resources for businesses and developers to execute their vision. The expansive C-3 Highway Commercial zoning offers limitless possibilities. Whether for the development of a flagship retail center, auto services, fuel stations, or light manufacturing, this site serves as an ideal canvas for growth. These factors make the property a truly compelling choice for forward-thinking investors and developers.
 
Ideally located amid renowned retailers, restaurants, hotels, and essential amenities, this property offers immediate access to a vibrant consumer base. The land is directly beside the TA Truck Stop and across the street from the Pilot Truck Stop. Leading brands such as Walgreens, Jimmy John’s, and Ace Hardware are in the immediate vicinity, while high-traffic destinations like Taco Bell, Cracker Barrel, Dollar Tree, and Starbucks further amplify the area’s appeal. Several lodging and hotel options sit across the interstate, drawing in additional travelers to the area. Its proximity to major interstates, including being situated directly beside a primary commuter route, and less than 18 miles from Downtown St. Louis, makes this location especially attractive for investors and businesses seeking visibility and convenience.
 
Strategically positioned within the greater St. Louis market, the largest metropolitan area in Missouri with nearly 2.8 million residents, this property offers investors unparalleled access to a vibrant and diverse economy. The region’s central location and robust infrastructure support established industries like manufacturing, healthcare, and financial services, fueling ongoing growth and transformation. Major employers such as BJC Health System, Washington University, Boeing, SSM Health, and Mercy anchor the local economy, while Boeing’s recent expansion underscores continued investment and job creation in advanced manufacturing. Leading financial institutions, including Edward Jones, Stifel Financial, and Wells Fargo, maintain significant regional operations, further enhancing stability and opportunity for long-term success.

brokerDetails.name: Luke Allan
brokerDetails.tagLine: Broker Associate specializing in commercial, land,  and income properties
brokerDetails.title: Broker Associate
brokerDetails.bio:
Luke has been involved in real estate since he was 12 years-old, selling properties at auction with his family business, Allan Auction Service. In high school, he worked summers at the Madison County Assessor’s office, which led him to become a licensed real estate appraiser in the 1990’s.

During the past 25 years, he has worked as a licensed Broker marketing and managing investment property. His ability to communicate with property owners, clients, and city officials helps him to provide superior customer service. Being a lifelong resident of Madison County, and working in St. Clair County during the past two decades, his knowledge and experience in the Metro East market is an asset to his clients and the Kunkel Wittenauer Group team.

Luke resides in Troy with his wife Beth, and his three children. When he is away from the office, he enjoys boating and working on his classic cars.

description:
Prime Development exclusively presents The Rio Ranch Residential Lots in Liberty Hill, ready to be developed in this up-and-coming corridor of Williamson County. These private and spacious lots offer stunning views, landscaping, and exclusive access to nature trails around the North San Gabriel River. Ready to build on with minimal setups required, Rio Ranch is perfect for developers itching to make their footprint just minutes from the Texas Triangle. 
   
The well-established community of Santa Rita Ranch perfectly neighbors 3150 County Road 258, positioning these lots in one of the most desirable areas of Central Texas. The Rio Ranch Residential lots are a 10-minute drive from top-ranking amenities such as Walmart Supercenter, Lowe’s Home Improvement, H-E-B, McDonald’s, Starbucks, and the hot retail hub of Leander, Texas. 3150 County Road 258 enjoys smooth accessibility, with prime arterial routes like Highway 29, US 183, and Interstate 25 encompassing the lots.
